arguments.task: Research the app LiveAgent for API buildability. Prefer official first-party sources only where possible. Visit LiveAgent official developer/API/auth/pricing pages and official GitHub or MCP registry pages if linked by official sources or clearly authoritative. Extract and verify: 1) one-line product description, 2) API existence and type (REST, GraphQL, both, or none), 3) API auth methods documented, 4) whether API access appears self-serve free/trial/paid/admin approval/partner gated, 5) pricing or plan requirements relevant to API, 6) rate limit notes, 7) whether an MCP server/integration exists, 8) notable signals like API docs breadth, SDKs, changelog, status pages. Return concise structured notes with source URLs for each claim. If a page is unavailable, use another official page. Do not use blog posts unless first-party and directly relevant. If evidence is ambiguous, say so explicitly.

input.thought: Launch browser research on official LiveAgent sources for API buildability evidence.

raw_result.description: All-in-one customer service platform with AI chatbot, live chat, ticketing, and call center.
raw_result.auth_notes: Pricing comparison explicitly lists "API keys" by plan, indicating key-based API authentication; browser task could not access the API reference to verify exact header/query usage.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing evidence indicates API keys are included on paid plans only, with 1 key on Small, 5 on Medium, 10 on Large, and 100 on Enterprise.

api_surface.breadth: Support evidence indicates a complete API reference exists, but the accessible browser session could not open the reference itself, so endpoint breadth is only partially verified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No MCP server evidence was verified from the gathered sources; this remains uncertain and should be human-reviewed.

raw_result.blocker: API exists and paid self-serve access appears available, but the official API reference was inaccessible in-browser during research, leaving authentication details and endpoint breadth partially unverified.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit documentation was verified from the gathered official pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official support portal surfaced a link labeled "Complete API reference" and a developer changelog link pointing to dev.ladesk.com; official pricing exposes API key allotments by plan, which is a strong signal of maintained API access.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Key technical fields remain partially unverified because the browser session encountered connection errors opening the API reference/postman pages. REST type and lack of MCP evidence are inferred from support-page labels and absence of verified docs, not fully confirmed from the API documentation itself.
